steps.title: Compare the methods
steps.content: The researcher argues that GPS records all movements, so it should produce much higher travel distances than round-trip distance for the same population. The example should therefore show an RTD result lower than the GPS result for the same animal.

steps.title: Use one population
steps.content: Choice A predicts a GPS value lower than a comparison point, which runs against the argument. Choice C gives only a weak lower bound for caribou under RTD. Choice D makes an unsupported prediction about Tibetan antelope. The remaining choice directly says the caribou RTD value would be lower than its GPS value. The answer is B.
